name: Bobby Darin
abstract: Walden Robert Cassotto (May 14, 1936 – December 20, 1973), known by the stage name Bobby Darin, was an American singer, songwriter, and actor who performed pop, swing, folk, rock and roll and country music. Darin started his career as a songwriter for Connie Francis.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Bobby Darin and discusses his hit 'Mack the Knife', but the interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not respond or provide any information indicating a personal or professional relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Caramanica
about_quotes.evidence: Another story, this guy was in the main room with Bobby Darin, and it was the same situation, they heard this drummer and he became Bobby Darin’s drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Caramanica interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, July 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I did Bobby Darin's first big hit record, "Splish Splash"... I did "Splash Splash" with Bobby Darin, I did "Calendar Girl" with Bobby Darin

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: Roger was Bobby Darin’s musical director in the late 60s and wrote prolifically for television and film.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: I stayed there for four years. I did it for four years with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...and Bobby Darin, people like that. That really changed the perspective of what ... and Bill Haley, we talked about that.
